pages.raw_text:
For the past week members of the
Western Pine Shippers association
have been attempting to arrange a
date with the representatives of the
various railway lines. having for an
object discussion of rates from
western points to the eastern dis
tricts. 1t is claimed they have a
great surplus Of lumber out here
and an effort will be made to obtain
a reduced rate on all lines.


8. q. Fulton. assistant general
freight agent of the Northern Paci-
fic; J. c. Eden. western traffic agent
Of the Great Northern, of Seattle.
and r. b. Miller. general freight
agent Of the o. r. & NJ, have all
agreed to meet the shippers and the
date that will very likely be agreed
upon Is September 81. II this special
rate can be given the shippers. the
lumber that will be sent east within
the next few months will eclipse all
past shipments
